Container.Name: domestique

Branch.Tag: latest: current main branch. Version tags (e.g. v0.1.035) are also published if you want to pin a specific build.

Container.Overview:
Quietly files completed bike-race torrent downloads (autobrr, Transmission, or a manual drop) into a Plex-friendly library layout, renaming them into a standardized "Show - SYYYYEnn - Title - ptNN.ext" scheme. Copies only, never moves or deletes from your download share, so seeding is unaffected. See the project's README for full setup: Transmission's script-torrent-done hook, optional Plex partial-scan refresh, optional hot-folder ingestion, optional Discord notifications, and a password-gated web UI for editing show definitions and testing the matcher.
IMPORTANT: on first run, "Config File" below is auto-seeded from the image's own bundled config/events.json (currently the maintainer's own road-racing race list, several hundred entries) - open the web UI (set WEBUI_PASSWORD below) or edit that file directly to prune it down to the races your own tracker actually covers, or add ones it's missing.
Container.Requires: A downloads share (from Transmission or similar) and a Plex media library share. Both are plain host folders you already have - see each Path config's description below for how to find the right values.

Config.Name: WebUI / Webhook / Health Port
Config.Description: Port the web UI (/ui), the Transmission webhook (/webhook/torrent-done), and the health check (/health) all listen on.

Config.Name: Library Root
Config.Description: Your Plex media library root - point this at your actual racing library folder. Renamed/copied files land here.

Config.Name: LIBRARY_ROOT
Config.Description: The container-side path the app uses for the library (must match Library Root's fixed mount point above - you shouldn't need to change this).

Config.Name: Downloads Share (read-only)
Config.Description: The SAME share Transmission's own container maps to /downloads internally - check Transmission's Docker template path mappings to confirm what that is. This is the whole share (not just its 'complete' subfolder), mounted read-only since Domestique only ever copies from here.

Config.Name: Hot-folder (read-write, optional)
Config.Description: Optional read-write overlay on a 'domestique' subfolder of the share above, for hot-folder ingestion (drop a file/folder here to bypass Transmission entirely). Only takes effect if HOTFOLDER_DIR below is also set to /downloads/domestique; otherwise this is just an unused empty folder.

Config.Name: Config File
Config.Description: Persists your show/event definitions across container updates and image upgrades. Auto-created from the image's bundled config/events.json the first time this file doesn't exist yet (see the Overview above) - edit it by hand or through the web UI afterward.

Config.Name: CONFIG_PATH
Config.Description: Where the app looks for the config file above, from inside the container. Matches the Config File mount target - no need to change this.

Config.Name: Settings File
Config.Description: Persists your Plex/Discord/hot-folder settings across container updates. Auto-created (seeded from the PLEX_*/DISCORD_*/HOTFOLDER_* variables below) the first time this file doesn't exist yet - edit it live afterward through the web UI's Settings panel instead of these variables.

Config.Name: SETTINGS_PATH
Config.Description: Where the app looks for the settings file above, from inside the container. Matches the Settings File mount target - no need to change this.

Config.Name: PLEX_URL
Config.Description: One-time seed only: used to create Settings File above if it doesn't exist yet, then ignored - edit Plex settings live afterward via the web UI. Enables a Plex partial-scan refresh after each successful archive. e.g. http://192.168.1.10:32400

Config.Name: PLEX_TOKEN
Config.Description: One-time seed only (see PLEX_URL above). Your Plex auth token, required if PLEX_URL is set. See the README's Plex section for how to find it.

Config.Name: PLEX_SECTION_ID
Config.Description: One-time seed only (see PLEX_URL above). The racing library's Plex section id, required if PLEX_URL is set.

Config.Name: PLEX_LIBRARY_ROOT
Config.Description: One-time seed only (see PLEX_URL above). Only needed if Plex's own container sees this library at a different internal path than Library Root above (the same category of issue as the Downloads Share mapping).

Config.Name: HOTFOLDER_DIR
Config.Description: One-time seed only: used to create Settings File above if it doesn't exist yet, then ignored - edit hot-folder settings live afterward via the web UI. Set to /downloads/domestique to enable hot-folder ingestion (matches the read-write mount above). Leave unset to disable.

Config.Name: HOTFOLDER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS
Config.Description: One-time seed only (see HOTFOLDER_DIR above). Tuning: how often (ms) to check a hot-folder drop for size/mtime stability before processing it.

Config.Name: HOTFOLDER_STABLE_POLLS
Config.Description: One-time seed only (see HOTFOLDER_DIR above). Tuning: consecutive stable polls required before a hot-folder drop is treated as done.

Config.Name: DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL
Config.Description: One-time seed only: used to create Settings File above if it doesn't exist yet, then ignored - edit Discord settings live afterward via the web UI. Posts a summary message to a Discord channel after every torrent-done event. Create one via the target channel's Settings, Integrations, Webhooks.

Config.Name: DISCORD_MENTION_USER_ID
Config.Description: One-time seed only (see DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL above). A Discord user id to @mention, but only on review-worthy notifications (auto-created shows, warnings, errors). Leave unset to have every notification post without a mention.

Config.Name: WEBUI_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Set this to enable the web UI at /ui (events editor, match tester, upload, activity log). Fails CLOSED, not open: leaving this unset keeps /ui and /api/* returning 503, since that surface can read and overwrite your config.

Config.Name: WEBUI_USER
Config.Description: Optional: also require this exact username (HTTP Basic Auth) alongside WEBUI_PASSWORD. Leave unset to accept any username, checking only the password.
